live_path.role: CLI entrypoint parses --owner and forwards it to the service layer
Orchestrates owner resolution, record creation, routing-key derivation, and payload emission
Derives effective owner and owner_source from explicit CLI input or defaults
Builds the stored base record with name, status, and resolved owner only
Computes routing_key from the resolved owner and item name after owner resolution
Emits the final payload by copying the record and injecting owner_source and routing_key

owner_source.logic: Returns "explicit" when owner is present after strip(); otherwise returns "default" with the configured default owner.

routing_key.logic: Slugifies owner and name, then joins them as <owner>:<name>.
routing_key.ordering: Computed in sync_app.service.sync_item after _resolve_owner and after the CLI has already forwarded --owner.

emission.logic: Copies the base record and appends owner_source and routing_key to the emitted payload.

test_observations.observation: Explicit owner input yields owner=pm-oncall, owner_source=explicit, routing_key=pm-oncall:launch-checklist.
Missing --owner falls back to config/defaults.json owner and marks owner_source as default before emission.
CLI path preserves --owner through sync_app.cli.main into emitted payload fields.

rejected_decoys.reason: Legacy helper that writes owner_source into a record, but it is not called by sync_app.service.sync_item or sync_app.cli.main.
Unused legacy routing-key builder; live code imports and calls sync_app.serializer.build_routing_key instead.
Fallback reader from a record, but no live caller uses it for emitted payload construction.
